elements.sent-2411-001: We introduce a generalisation of norm relations in the group algebra $\Q[G]$, where $G$ is a finite group.
elements.sent-2411-002: We give some properties of these relations, and use them to obtain relations between the $S$-unit groups of different subfields of the same Galois extension of $\Q$, of Galois group $G$.
elements.sent-2411-003: Then we deduce an algorithm to compute the class groups of some number fields by reducing the problem to fields of lower degree.
elements.sent-2411-004: We compute the class groups of some large number fields.
elements.sent-2411-005: The problem of computing the class group of number fields is a central problem in number theory, with applications for example in cryptography or in class field theory.
elements.sent-2411-007: The most commonly used method to compute the class group or the $S$-units groups of $K$ is Buchmann's algorithm \cite{Buchman .}
elements.sent-2411-008: Its complexity grows quickly with the degree~$n$: if we denote by $D(K)$ the discriminant of $K$, the time complexity of this algorithm for fixed~$n$ is in $\O( e^{a \sqrt{ \ln|D(K)|\ln \ln|D(K)| )$ where $a$ is a constant, and the implicit constant of the $\O$ depends on $n$ exponentially; note in addition that the discriminant grows at least exponentially with~$n$.}}
elements.sent-2411-009: So it would be beneficial to have an inductive method to reduce these problems to similar ones in number fields of smaller degree and discriminant.
elements.sent-2411-010: Such inductive methods have already been proposed.
elements.sent-2411-011: Suppose $K/F$ is a Galois extension of number fields, of Galois group $G$.
elements.sent-2411-012: By studying relations between the subgroups of $G$ arising from character theory, we can find corresponding relations between the arithmetic invariants of the intermediate fields.
elements.sent-2411-013: For a subgroup $H< G$, denote by $\Ind_{G/H (1_H)$ the permutation character of $G$ induced from the trivial representation of~$H$.}
elements.sent-2411-014: A Brauer relation \cite{Brauer is a relation of the form \begin{equation}\sum_{H<G} a_H \Ind_{G/H}(1_H) = 0\end{equation} with $a_H \in \Z$.}
elements.sent-2411-015: Brauer proved that when such a relation exists, there is a corresponding relation between arithmetic invariants of the fields~$K^H$.
elements.sent-2411-016: In~\cite{norm , Biasse, Fieker, Hofmann and Page studied another type of relation called norm relation.}
elements.sent-2411-017: Given a subgroup $H < G$, we define its norm element to be the formal sum $N_H = \sum_{h \in H h$ in $\Z[G]$.}
elements.sent-2411-018: If $R$ is a commutative ring, $\HH$ a set of subgroups of $G$, a norm relation over $R$ with respect to $\HH$ is an equality in $R[G]$ of the form \begin{equation}1 = \sum_{i = 1}^\ell a_i N_{H_i} b_i\end{equation} with $a_i, b_i \in R[G]$ and $H_i<G$.
elements.sent-2411-019: In their paper, they derive from such a relation an inductive algorithm to compute the class group or the groups of $S$-units of $K$ by reducing the problem to a similar problem on the subfields $K^H$.
elements.sent-2411-020: Our goal is to generalize the notion of norm relation in order to be able to use the same kind of method to compute the class group of some number fields $K$ even without a Galois extension $K/F$.
elements.sent-2411-021: If $K$ is a number field, let us denote by $\Tilde{K}$ its Galois closure, and let $G$ be its Galois group (i.e. the Galois group of the extension $\Tilde{K /\Q$).}
elements.sent-2411-022: Let $H$ be the subgroup of $G$ such that $K = \Tilde{K ^H$.}
elements.sent-2411-023: Let $R$ be a commutative ring, and $J_1, \cdots, J_l$ some subgroups of $G$.
elements.sent-2411-024: A generalised norm relation over $R$ with respect to $H$ and the $J_i$ is an equality in $R[G]$ of the form \begin{equation}N_H = \sum_{i = 1}^\ell a_i N_{J_i} b_i\end{equation} with $a_i, b_i \in R[G]$.
elements.sent-2411-025: Classical norm relation are a special case of generalised norm relation where $H$ is the trivial group.
elements.sent-2411-026: Given $K$ and subfields~$K_i$ of $\Tilde{K}$, we say that $K$ admits a generalised norm relation with respect to the $K_i$ if~$G$ admits one over $\Q$ with respect to $H$ and $\{J_i\}$, where the $J_i$ are such that $K_i = \Tilde{K ^{J_i}$ for all~$i$.}
